title: Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole

seo.title: Authentic Oaxacan Mole Negro Recipe - Black Mole
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Oaxacan Mole Negro with our traditional black mole recipe. Rich, complex, and deeply flavorful, this Mexican classic is perfect for dinner. Get the step-by-step guide!

Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Oaxacan Mole Negro with chicken, garnished with sesame seeds and cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich, complex flavors of Oaxaca with this traditional Mole Negro, a black mole sauce perfect for chicken or turkey.

description: Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole is a masterpiece of Mexican cuisine, a complex and deeply flavorful sauce with a rich, dark hue. This traditional dish combines an array of dried chiles, nuts, spices, fruits, and a touch of chocolate, creating an unparalleled sweet, smoky, and savory experience. It is often served with chicken or turkey, making for an unforgettable dinner.

instructions.title: Prepare and Hydrate Chiles
instructions.text: Quickly toast chiles in a dry skillet over medium heat for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn them. Transfer to a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them soften for 10 minutes. Drain, reserving some of the chile water for later.

instructions.title: Toast and Fry Remaining Ingredients
instructions.text: While chiles hydrate, quickly toast almonds, peanuts, sesame seeds, cinnamon stick, cloves, peppercorns, and cumin in the same dry skillet for about 3 minutes until fragrant. Set aside. In a separate pan, quickly sauté chopped onion, garlic, halved tomatoes, and tomatillos in 1 tablespoon oil for about 4 minutes until slightly softened. In a small pan, fry torn tortilla pieces and plantain slices in 1 tablespoon oil until golden, about 3 minutes. Drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Paste
instructions.text: Combine the softened chiles, toasted nuts and spices, sautéed aromatics, fried tortillas and plantain, and raisins with 1 cup of warm chicken broth in a blender. Blend until a very smooth paste forms, adding a little reserved chile water or more broth if needed to achieve a thick, pourable consistency.

instructions.title: Sauté the Mole Paste
instructions.text: Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of lard or oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the blended mole paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 15 minutes. This step is crucial for deepening the flavor and color of the mole, allowing it to darken and thicken significantly.

instructions.title: Simmer and Finish the Mole
instructions.text: Gradually stir in the remaining 3 cups of warm chicken broth, chopped Mexican chocolate, sugar, and salt to taste. Bring the mole to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low and continue to cook, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes. The mole should thicken further and all the flavors will meld beautifully. Adjust seasoning as needed.

instructions.title: Serve Your Authentic Mole Negro
instructions.text: Ladle the rich and complex Mole Negro over cooked chicken or turkey pieces. Garnish with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds and fresh cilantro. Serve immediately with warm corn tortillas and a side of Mexican rice for a complete and unforgettable Oaxacan dinner.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, always toast your chiles and spices before blending. This unlocks their full aroma and depth, crucial for a rich mole and cannot be skipped.
While a variety of chiles are best, you can substitute Pasilla Oaxaca with more Ancho or Mulato chiles if unavailable. Dont skip the Mexican chocolate; it adds a unique depth, not just sweetness.
Mole Negro freezes beautifully. Make a larger batch and store portions for quick, flavorful meals. Reheat gently, adding a splash of broth or water if needed to adjust consistency.
article:
Oaxaca, Mexico, is a land steeped in culinary tradition, where food is a vibrant expression of culture and history. Among its many gastronomic treasures, one stands supreme, revered for its complexity, depth, and rich heritage: Mole Negro. This dark, velvety sauce is more than just a dish; it's a symphony of flavors, a testament to generations of culinary artistry. If you're looking for an authentic Mole Negro Recipe to transport your taste buds straight to the heart of Mexico, you've come to the right place. Prepare to embark on a culinary adventure that will redefine your understanding of Mexican dinner recipes.

## What is Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole?
Mole Negro, often simply called "Black Mole," is the undisputed king of Oaxacan moles, considered the most intricate and celebratory of them all. Unlike other moles that might lean sweeter or spicier, Mole Negro achieves a profound balance through a meticulous blend of dozens of ingredients. Its signature deep, almost obsidian color comes from the specific combination of charred chiles, burnt tortillas, and often, a touch of dark chocolate.

At its heart, Mole Negro is a sauce composed of several types of dried chiles (usually chilhuacle negro, mulato, pasilla mexicano, and ancho), along with a rich array of nuts (almonds, peanuts), seeds (sesame, pumpkin), spices (cinnamon, cloves, cumin, black pepper, anise), fruits (raisins, plantains), herbs, garlic, onion, and a touch of Mexican chocolate. Each ingredient plays a crucial role, contributing to layers of smoky, sweet, spicy, earthy, and savory notes that unfold with every bite. It’s a labor of love, traditionally prepared for significant celebrations like weddings, baptisms, and Day of the Dead, symbolizing community, tradition, and the bountiful harvest. Mastering this Mole Negro Recipe connects you with a profound culinary legacy.

## Ingredients You Need
Gathering the right ingredients is vital for an authentic Mole Negro Recipe. While the list may seem extensive, each component is important for achieving the true flavor profile.

For the Chiles (approx. 15-20 total):
*   4-5 large Chilhuacle Negro chiles
